rationale.R1.1: An academic abstract's strength is substantially derived from how crisply it frames the gap and contribution claim; 'make this stronger' applied here almost certainly involves tightening or elevating the thesis.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an overt internal contradiction: §2 asserts the router is 'stateless across queries' and attributes the closed-form derivation to this property, yet §3 describes a sliding window over the 10,000 most recent queries maintaining histograms refreshed every 500 queries — a stateful mechanism. This contradiction directly weakens the text and falls squarely under logical consistency.
rationale.R1.6: The most common lay interpretation of 'make this stronger' on prose is improved writing quality — sharper sentences, tighter paragraphs, more forceful rhetoric — making writing polish a central expected responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I prioritize prose and rhetorical strength (sharper sentences, more forceful argument) or conceptual and logical strengthening (hardening the contribution claim and resolving the stateless/sliding-window contradiction)?
